title: [DO NOT MERGE] Test Buildah v1.41.0 vendor
body:
Don't merge this, don't even think about it!

Test bumping Buildah to v1.41.0

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Looks like some annotation man page changes from Buildah did not make it into Podman yet, will dig.
I think I've the necessary additions to the man pages now.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Ugh, got the man pages happy, now I'm running into test change issues with the Buildah changes.

The errors that I'm encountering are all like this:

```
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: buildah inspect --format {{printf "%q" .Docker.Config.Labels}} scratch-image
   #| expected: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.41.0-dev" "test":"label"]'
   #|   actual: 'map["io.buildah.version":"1.41.0" "test":"label"]'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

Where the version of Buildah returned from `podman inspect` is coming back as 1.41.0, but the one from 'buildah version` is coming back as 1.41.0-dev.  I strongly suspect this is something that will go away with a proper vendoring and I'd like to lobby to merge the Buildah PR, and then add in the man page changes from here when I vendor Buildah 1.41.0 into here.
